news.title: North Koreans behind nearly half of US tech industry hacks, says CrowdStrike
news.summary: North Koreans hackers posing as remote IT workers and recruiters remain a major threat to U.S., European, and Asian companies, accounting for about half of all attacks over the past 12 months.

news.title: Wing drone delivery might not be a novelty anymore
news.summary: Wing is expanding into seven more U.S. cities through its partnership with Walmart.

news.summary: The media giant is pushing to expand its mobile and gaming business.

news.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
news.summary: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

news.title: The Indian government got cold feet on Starlink just before SpaceX’s IPO
news.summary: Problems with Starlink's India expansion could challenge SpaceX's IPO growth story.

news.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions

news.title: Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
news.summary: New college graduates around the country have been booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI. Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out. news.title: Google won’t just admit it’s feeding YouTube creators to its music AI
news.summary: If you've uploaded a song to YouTube, Google almost certainly considers your video fair game for training its Lyria music AI, it just won't admit it right now. A group of independent musicians is suing Google, claiming that it illegally used songs they uploaded to YouTube to train its Lyria 3 model.

news.title: You can just tell the Instagram algorithm what you want now
news.summary: Instagram is going to let you tweak what its algorithm shows you on your main feed. With the Your Algorithm feature, "you can now see the topics we think you're interested in, and change them, across all the major parts of Instagram," according to Instagram boss Adam Mosseri. news.title: Wrongful Arrest Exposes Failures in One of the Oldest Police Face-Recognition Tools in the US
news.summary: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.

news.title: China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
news.summary: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
